question_text: Compare two waves: Wave A has a high amplitude and low frequency; Wave B has a low amplitude and high frequency. For a sound wave, which would be louder and which would have a higher pitch? Explain your answers.
correct_answer: Wave A would be louder (because amplitude determines loudness) and Wave B would have a higher pitch (because frequency determines pitch). Loudness depends on amplitude, pitch depends on frequency—they are independent properties.
explanation: Amplitude and frequency control different properties of sound. Amplitude (height of wave) affects how loud it is—a bigger wave is louder. Frequency (how many waves per second) affects pitch—more waves per second means a higher pitch. You can have a loud low note or a quiet high note.
